title: The Lay Folks Mass Book and offices according to the Use of York (Simmons selection)
summary: The repository artifact is a selective encoding of Thomas Frederick Simmons's 1879 EETS volume, not an encoding of one medieval codex or one homogeneous medieval work. U-M retained the four parallel Lay Folks' Mass Book texts B, C, E, and F with readings from A and D; four York bidding prayers; the York Hours of the Cross; Appendices III-V; and selected notes and fragments. It omitted large portions of Simmons's preface, introduction, appendices, notes, glossary, and index. Simmons worked through named nineteenth-century transcripts for the principal LFMB witnesses, rearranged the disordered E text against B, expanded manuscript abbreviations in italics, marked supplied text and damage typographically, and represented rubrication in Clarendon type. His encoded notes also quote Lumby's 1870 edition of the Vertewis of the Mess from Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5. The encoded selection maps to discontiguous printed pages 2-60, 64-80, 82-87, 122-154, and 367-371, plus two unnumbered part pages. Complete provider objects have been cached for the 1879 edition, Lumby's 1870 supporting edition, NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1, the Vernon Manuscript, and Harvard MS Widener 1; each local copy maps the relevant portion inside the full object. The Harvard object is a complete 182-image microfilm/provider inventory but is not a physically complete surrogate because exterior boards, spine, and edge views are not exposed.
review_notes: The Lay Folks' Mass Book is Simmons's Victorian title. Modern scholarship treats the nine surviving medieval copies as distinct versions rather than transparent witnesses to one recoverable wording.

Simmons's four main LFMB text streams derive from witnesses B, C, E, and F. A and D supply selected readings only. G, H, and I were identified after 1879 and are collateral witnesses, not ancestors of CME00052.

Appendix IV is mapped inside the complete Vernon codex at fols. 302v-303v, IIIF canvases 491-493. The NLS A fragment is mapped at fols. 57r-58v, pages/canvases 129-132. Bidding Prayer II is mapped inside MS Widener 1 at fols. 105r-107r, IIIF sequences 110-112.

The whole-object rule is applied to manuscript facsimiles whenever the provider exposes the whole object. Completeness statements distinguish provider-object coverage from physical-object surface coverage.
Medieval text, nineteenth-century edition, modern encoding, scholarly article, repository artifact, and manuscript-image rights are recorded as separate layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00052
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00052
entities.description: A public electronic text selectively encoding Simmons 1879. Its table of contents exposes the four LFMB versions, bidding prayers, York Hours, Appendices III-V, and a collection of fragments.
notes: A 2016 revision note in the XML records structural review, removal of prose fragments, and changes to multiple element structures.
Repeated PB elements across parallel streams are print-page milestones, not distinct manuscript leaves.

entities.label: Simmons's 1879 Lay Folks Mass Book
alternate_labels: The Lay Folks Mass Book; or, The Manner of Hearing Mass
entities.description: The immediate source edition for U-M. The encoded subset spans the LFMB parallel-text section, four bidding prayers, the York Hours, Appendices III-V, and selected notes and fragments.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Frederick Simmons, ed., The Lay Folks Mass Book; or, The Manner of Hearing Mass, EETS Original Series 71 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1879).
bibliographic.title: The Lay Folks Mass Book; or, The Manner of Hearing Mass, with Rubrics and Devotions for the People, in Four Texts, and Offices in English according to the Use of York
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co.

notes: CME00052 includes edition pages 2-60, 64-80, 82-87, 122-154, and 367-371, plus the unnumbered four-text list and York Hours part title; these correspond to PDF pages 79-138, 142-165, 200-232, and 445-449 in the cached Internet Archive scan, with gaps.
On pp. 367-371 Simmons compares the printed fragments with the Vertewis of the Mess and explicitly cites Lumby's Ratis Raving edition at p. 113, line 3943.
Simmons's title-page phrase 'from manuscripts of the Xth to the XVth century' describes the volume's broad source range, not the date range of LFMB itself.

entities.label: Lumby's 1870 Ratis Raving
alternate_labels: Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
entities.description: The printed source explicitly cited by Simmons for comparison passages from the Vertewis of the Mess in his encoded notes. Lumby edited the collection from Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5; the relevant work is printed on pp. 113-115.

bibliographic.citation: J. Rawson Lumby, ed., Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, EETS Original Series 43 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1870).
bibliographic.title: Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co.

notes: The cached 528-page scan is the complete provider volume; the Lumby title page is PDF p. 9, its manuscript statement is PDF p. 11, and the relevant Vertewis of the Mess is PDF pp. 131-133.

entities.label: E. Brock transcript of Royal MS 17 B XVII
entities.description: A working transcript prepared for EETS; Simmons edited it, collated proofs against the manuscript, and consulted Edward Maunde Thompson in doubtful cases.

entities.label: George Parker transcript of Corpus Christi College MS 155
entities.description: A working transcript made from the manuscript; Parker also collated the edition proofs against it.

entities.label: EETS transcript of Gonville and Caius MS 84/166 procured by Dennis Hall
entities.description: A working transcript procured for EETS by Dennis Hall, who collated the proof with the manuscript; the copyist is not named by Simmons.

entities.label: Walter W. Skeat transcript of the Yates Thompson LFMB
entities.description: Skeat's transcript of text F; Skeat also corrected proof. The manuscript is now Newnham College MS 900.4.

entities.label: T. Milville Raven transcript of LFMB witness A
entities.description: Raven's transcript of the NLS fragment, examined by Cosmo Innes and used for selected readings rather than a complete printed text.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: A late fifteenth-century Derbyshire miscellany. Its fragmentary LFMB version occupies fols. 57r-58v and supplied selected readings to Simmons.

physical_description.extent: LFMB fragment at fols. 57r-58v; complete digital object has 482 canvases
notes: The LFMB begins and ends within the four mapped surfaces.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 B XVII
entities.description: The oldest and fullest LFMB version used as Simmons's principal text B, at fols. 3r-13r.

physical_description.extent: LFMB at fols. 3r-13r; codex of 108 leaves
notes: Simmons dated the LFMB hand about 1375.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Oxford MS 155
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century Rievaulx Abbey manuscript whose LFMB version occupies fols. 252v-268r.

notes: The codex belonged to Rievaulx Abbey and names Abbot William Spenser.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.5.31
entities.description: A northern revised LFMB version at fols. 1r-5v. Simmons printed selected readings; Gerould edited it in full in 1904.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 84/166, part II
entities.description: An independent, heavily transposed West Midland LFMB version on pages 173-179 of the bound volume. Simmons rearranged it to B's order.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: early to mid-fifteenth century; associated calendar begins 1443

entities.label: Newnham College MS 900.4
entities.description: A revised LFMB version appended to a Piers Plowman manuscript, at fols. 104v-109v.

physical_description.extent: LFMB at fols. 104v-109v; written on eleven formerly blank pages following Piers Plowman

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9
entities.description: A fifteenth-century radical revision of LFMB at fols. 55v-60r, identified after Simmons and therefore collateral to the 1879 edition.

entities.label: University of Liverpool MS F.4.9
entities.description: An early fifteenth-century Yorkshire LFMB version at fols. 203v-207v, identified after Simmons.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 36523
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century LFMB version at fols. 88r-93r, identified after Simmons.

entities.label: Houghton Library MS Widener 1
entities.description: A York ritual/manual dated from a paschal table beginning in 1403. Simmons's Bidding Prayer II comes from fols. 105r-107r and ends partway through fol. 107r in CME00052.

physical_description.extent: 176 leaves, bound; Bidding Prayer II at fols. 105r-107r
notes: Bound by Riviere in dark red morocco and housed in a slipcase; the microfilm does not expose all physical binding surfaces.

entities.label: York Minster Library Fothergill York Manual
entities.description: An early fifteenth-century York Manual of 177 folios used by Simmons for Bidding Prayer III and for the later added Bidding Prayer IV. The current shelfmark and exact folio span were not established from the public catalog.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde's 1509 York Manual
alternate_labels: Manuale quoddam secundum usum matris ecclesie Eboracensis
entities.description: The first printed York Manual and the source Simmons names for Bidding Prayer V. A Bodleian copy survives as 4° Z 13 Th.Seld.; no open whole-copy facsimile was found.

bibliographic.citation: Manuale quoddam secundum usum matris ecclesie Eboracensis (London: Wynkyn de Worde for John Gaschet and Jacques Ferrebouc, 9 February 1509).
bibliographic.title: Manuale quoddam secundum usum matris ecclesie Eboracensis
bibliographic.publisher: Wynkyn de Worde for John Gaschet and Jacques Ferrebouc

entities.label: York Minster Library MS XVI.K.6
entities.description: A c. 1420 York book of hours. Simmons printed its consecutive English Hours of the Cross from fols. 1r-2v and Latin Hours passages dispersed through the codex, supplementing omissions from printed Hours and other sources.

physical_description.extent: 120 leaves; English Hours at fols. 1r-2v; related Latin texts at fols. 2v, 3v, 13v, 16r, 18v-20v, 22r, and 24r-v

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 1286
entities.description: The source of Simmons's Appendix III, a prose preparation for receiving the Eucharist at fols. 223r-226. It is not an LFMB witness; a former LALME claim to that effect is corrected by modern scholarship.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1
entities.description: The large Vernon miscellany. Simmons's Appendix IV, A Treatise of the Manner and Mede of the Mass, occupies fols. 302v-303v, official IIIF canvases 491-493.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i leaves, incomplete; work at fols. 302v-303v

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus A XXVI
entities.description: The source of Appendix V, Merita Missae attributed to John Lydgate. The XML's manuscript milestones run from fol. 154 through fol. 157.

physical_description.extent: 293 folios; Merita Missae at fols. 154r-157r approximately

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 3954
entities.description: Source of the opening of Lydgate's Virtutes Missarum printed in the encoded notes, at fol. 76 column 2.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson Poetry 36
entities.description: A comparison witness quoted in the encoded notes at fols. 6-9.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS 832
entities.description: A Latin comparison witness quoted in the encoded notes at fol. 168v; Simmons's legacy designation requires reconciliation with a current shelfmark.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2251
entities.description: Source of a final stanza quoted in the encoded notes at fol. 187v.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5
entities.description: The composite Cambridge manuscript from which Lumby edited Ratis Raving and ten other moral and religious pieces. The Vertewis of the Mess is item 11 of part 6 and occupies fols. 53r-54r, rendered by Lumby as fols. 53, 53b, and 54.

physical_description.extent: Composite manuscript in eight parts; relevant work is part 6, item 11, fols. 53r-54r (Lumby's 53/53b/54 notation)

entities.label: NLS complete facsimile of Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: A complete 482-page color surrogate beginning with the upper cover and ending with the lower cover. It includes all provider-exposed leaves and binding surfaces; no dedicated head, tail, fore-edge, or standalone spine view is exposed.

entities.label: Harvard microfilm facsimile of MS Widener 1
entities.description: The complete 182-image provider object, described by Harvard as digital images produced from microfilm of the entire work. It covers the textual leaf sequence and end material but is physically incomplete as a surrogate: exterior boards, spine, head, tail, and fore-edge views are not identifiable.
entities.reproduction_format: 182 microfilm-derived IIIF opening images; complete provider inventory, partial physical-object surface coverage
notes: The filming targets and all 182 manifest images are cached; completeness must not be read as color or preservation-master coverage.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: A complete 710-canvas color surrogate including covers, flyleaves, surviving leaves, and a final spine canvas. Dedicated edge views are not exposed.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases; complete local 1600-pixel bundle

entities.label: Smith 2021 LFMB manuscript study
entities.description: The modern scholarly basis for treating nine copies as distinct versions, dating and locating their transmission, and separating the post-Simmons G-I witnesses from the 1879 lineage.

bibliographic.citation: Jeremy J. Smith, 'The Manuscripts of the Middle English Lay Folks' Mass Book in Context,' Studia Anglica Posnaniensia 56(s1) (2021): 361-385.
bibliographic.title: The Manuscripts of the Middle English Lay Folks' Mass Book in Context

entities.label: Henderson's Manuale et processionale ad usum Eboracensis
entities.description: A supporting edition and description of surviving York manuals, including the former Lawson manuscript and the York Minster manuscript. It is not the immediate source of CME00052.

bibliographic.citation: William George Henderson, ed., Manuale et processionale ad usum insignis ecclesiae Eboracensis, Surtees Society 63 (Durham, 1875).
bibliographic.title: Manuale et processionale ad usum insignis ecclesiae Eboracensis

object.description: English Hours and dispersed Latin Hours
locators: English fols. 1r-2v; Latin fols. 2v-24v in discontiguous passages

scope.notes: Simmons supplemented missing Compline and Recommendation material from a Cambridge manuscript, a 1543 Prymer, and printed York Hours; the edition is not a diplomatic transcription of one source.

object.description: A Treatise of the Manner and Mede of the Mass

assertion.notes: The current shelfmark behind Simmons's abbreviated designation remains unresolved.

subject.description: comparative notes on virtues of hearing Mass, pp. 367-371

assertion.notes: Simmons explicitly cites Ratis Raving p. 113, line 3943 and quotes other passages from the same work.

object.description: complete provider-exposed manuscript and binding sequence

scope.notes: No standalone edge or dedicated spine views are exposed.

object.description: entire textual leaf sequence but incomplete physical-surface coverage

rights.jurisdiction: United States and ordinary copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: The underlying texts and medieval manuscript witnesses substantially predate modern copyright terms.
Published in 1879; Simmons died in 1884. U-M also marks the item public domain and No Copyright-United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive digital scan and reproduction layer
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata supplies no explicit license or rights statement for the scan layer; this does not alter the public-domain status of the 1879 edition's text.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and ordinary copyright jurisdictions

rights.component: Internet Archive digital scan and reproduction layer
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata supplies no explicit license or rights statement for the scan layer; this does not alter the public-domain status of Lumby's 1870 edition.
The exact U-M item says the materials are public domain and applies the No Copyright-United States rights statement.

rights.component: Repository XML serialization and encoding contributions
rights.basis: No explicit license file was found in the pinned CME submodule; public source access does not itself supply reuse terms for new encoding contributions.
The exact NLS object applies the Public Domain Mark to the manuscript work.

rights.component: NLS images and transcriptions on the exact object page, including medium downloads
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to exact-page exceptions
rights.basis: The NLS object page states that images and transcriptions may be used under CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise stated.
notes: Check whether the complete PDF deliverable carries any additional exception before redistributing it; attribution remains required.

rights.component: Microfilm-derived digital reproductions of the public-domain manuscript
rights.jurisdiction: United States and Harvard viewer terms
rights.basis: Harvard's viewer terms state that it does not assert copyright over digital reproductions of works in the U.S. public domain, subject to other identified restrictions; none was identified in the object record.
notes: Users remain responsible for applicable law and any non-copyright restriction; avoid implying Harvard endorsement or using Harvard marks.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photographs and cached IIIF images
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and CC BY-NC 4.0 license terms
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: Commercial publication or redistribution of images requires permission beyond CC BY-NC; the medieval text itself is a separate public-domain layer.

rights.basis: The Glasgow repository identifies the published version as CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and ordinary copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: Published in 1875 and outside modern copyright terms.

editorial_practices.description: Simmons states that italics expand contractions; CME00052 preserves many of these italic distinctions through HI elements.

editorial_practices.representation: Bracketed bold additions for damage and ordinary bracketed additions for supposed omissions
editorial_practices.description: Simmons distinguishes supplied text for physical damage from conjectural or omission-based additions; the encoding retains brackets and typographic markup unevenly.
Simmons used Clarendon type for rubrics or words written in red rather than reproducing color literally.
The edition presents B, C, E, and F in parallel, supplies selected readings from A and D, and was proof-collated against several manuscripts by Simmons or named collaborators.

editorial_practices.representation: Reordered to correspond with text B
editorial_practices.description: Simmons explicitly rearranged E's manuscript sequence for parallel comparison; the printed and encoded order is therefore editorial rather than codicological.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A 472-page scholarly edition with apparatus, glossary, and multiple appendices
editorial_practices.description: U-M encoded only the named page ranges and selected notes; absence from CME00052 must not be treated as absence from Simmons's volume.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME00052.xml.
evidence.summary: Establishes the exact encoded title, identifiers, structural counts, page milestones, named sources, manuscript folio milestones, editorial markup, and 2016 revision note.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00052 item information.
evidence.summary: Confirms title, London 1879 publication, public-domain and No Copyright-United States statements, subject terms, and the selected electronic contents.

evidence.citation: Thomas Frederick Simmons, ed., The Lay Folks Mass Book, EETS OS 71 (1879).
evidence.locator: title page; editor's preface pp. ix-xvi; introduction pp. xvii-lxxi; encoded pp. 2-60, 64-80, 82-87, 122-154, 367-371; notes pp. 346-352
evidence.summary: The immediate print source. It identifies manuscript witnesses and transcript intermediaries, explains collation and typography, supplies component boundaries and source loci, and states where the York Hours were supplemented from other witnesses.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, complete scan of Simmons 1879, item layfolksmassbook00simmuoft.
evidence.locator: 560 PDF pages; CME selection at PDF pp. 79-138, 142-165, 200-232, 445-449
evidence.summary: Provides the complete source volume and allows exact discontiguous page mapping. The item metadata supplies no explicit license for the scan layer.

evidence.citation: J. Rawson Lumby, ed., Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, EETS OS 43 (1870).
evidence.locator: title page at PDF p. 9; manuscript and contents statement at PDF pp. 11-12; Vertewis of the Mess at MS fols. 53r-54r, printed pp. 113-115, PDF pp. 131-133, lines 3917-3993
evidence.summary: Identifies Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5 as the manuscript edited, locates the Vertewis of the Mess as part 6 item 11, and supplies the exact passages Simmons cites and compares on pp. 367-371. The complete 528-page provider PDF is cached.

evidence.citation: Jeremy J. Smith, 'The Manuscripts of the Middle English Lay Folks' Mass Book in Context,' Studia Anglica Posnaniensia 56(s1) (2021): 361-385, doi:10.2478/stap-2021-0002.
evidence.summary: Current specialist account of all nine LFMB manuscripts, their exact loci, dates, dialectal and regional contexts, distinct versions, and post-Simmons discovery history. It corrects the claim that Ashmole 1286 is an LFMB witness and gives the article's CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, complete Adv.MS.19.3.1 digital object 133680060.
evidence.locator: complete 482 pages/canvases; LFMB A at pages/canvases 129-132, fols. 57r-58v
evidence.summary: Confirms the shelfmark and complete-book image object. Visual inspection fixes the four-page LFMB boundary. The object marks the manuscript Public Domain and states CC BY 4.0 terms for its images and transcriptions unless otherwise stated.

evidence.citation: British Library, Royal MS 17 B XVII catalog record.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, physical extent, date, and contents. The digitized-content field did not expose a complete public facsimile when checked.

evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark and manuscript identity; no complete public facsimile was exposed in the current record.

evidence.citation: British Library, Cotton MS Titus A XXVI catalog record.

evidence.summary: States that remote reprographics requests of up to 100 scans may be available subject to condition, copyright, and fee assessment.

evidence.citation: Harvard Library, Houghton Library MS Widener 1 catalog metadata.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, former Lawson ownership, c. 1403 date, 176 parchment leaves, binding, languages, and that microfilm-derived digital images are available for the entire work.

evidence.citation: Harvard Library IIIF Presentation manifest for MS Widener 1.
evidence.locator: 182 sequences; Bidding Prayer II at sequences 110-112, fols. 105r-107r
evidence.summary: Enumerates the full provider image object. Visual inspection confirms the prayer opening and encoded endpoint. The provider object lacks identifiable exterior-board, spine, and edge views, so physical-object completeness is partial.

evidence.citation: Alfred J. Horwood, 'A Manuscript Belonging to Sir John Lawson, Bart., of Brough Hall, Yorkshire,' Fifth Report of the Royal Commission on Historical Manuscripts (1876), Appendix, pp. 305-307.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 305-307; Bidding Prayer begins fol. 105 and next item begins fol. 107
evidence.summary: Provides a near-contemporary folio-by-folio description and transcribes the bidding prayer, fixing its beginning at fol. 105 and the following mass at fol. 107. Combined with the facsimile, it establishes the exact f. 107r endpoint.

evidence.citation: Harvard Library, Digitized Content Copyright and Viewer Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: States that Harvard does not assert copyright over digital reproductions of U.S. public-domain works, while requiring compliance with law and any item-specific restrictions.

evidence.citation: William George Henderson, ed., Manuale et processionale ad usum insignis ecclesiae Eboracensis (1875).
evidence.locator: preface pp. xv-xvi and York Manual text/variants
evidence.summary: Describes the former Lawson and York Minster manuals, their dates and extents, and publishes York Manual texts and collations. The complete supporting volume is cached.

evidence.citation: University of York, York Minster Library access and catalog guidance.
evidence.summary: Provides the current catalog, appointment, and reproduction-enquiry route for York Minster manuscripts; some paper catalogs remain necessary.

evidence.citation: Christie's, Manuale, use of York, Wynkyn de Worde, 9 February 1509, lot description.
evidence.summary: Confirms imprint, date, physical format, rarity, printed music, and surviving-copy history for the first printed York Manual.

evidence.citation: Usuarium, Manuale-processionale Eboracense 689.
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1509 York Manual, printer, Bodleian shelfmark 4° Z 13 Th.Seld., USTC number, and the supporting Henderson edition; its page links are to Henderson, not a facsimile of the 1509 copy.

evidence.citation: Birgitta Atlas, Bodleian MS Ashmole 1286 description.
evidence.summary: Provides an independent description of Ashmole 1286 and its physical extent. Simmons and the XML supply the exact Appendix III locus.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript), complete object 52f0a31a-1478-40e4-b05b-fddb1ad076ff.
evidence.locator: complete 710 canvases; A Treatise of the Manner and Mede of the Mass at fols. 302v-303v, canvases 491-493
evidence.summary: Confirms codex identity, date, extent, whole-object image sequence, exact work range, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image license. Visual inspection confirms work boundaries.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms and exact IIIF rights metadata.
evidence.summary: Explains attribution and noncommercial reuse conditions reflected by the exact object's CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

open_questions.question: What is the current shelfmark and exact folio range of the Fothergill York Manual used for Bidding Prayers III-IV?
next_steps: Ask York Minster Library to reconcile Simmons's and Henderson's description with its current MSS XVI catalog and provide the prayer folios.
If images are supplied, request the whole codex and map the prayers within it.

open_questions.question: Do complete public or requestable digital facsimiles exist for LFMB witnesses B-I and the uncached appendix and note witnesses?
next_steps: Send shelfmark-specific reproduction enquiries to the British Library, Corpus Christi College, CUL, Caius, Newnham, Liverpool, York Minster, and Bodleian.
Request whole manuscripts where feasible and map each relevant folio span inside the delivered object.

open_questions.question: Can any pre-archetypal source behind the English LFMB versions, including Simmons's proposed French work by 'dam Jeremy,' be securely identified?
next_steps: Treat Simmons's Rouen/French-source hypothesis as historical conjecture unless a current specialist source supplies new evidence.
Keep the lost or hypothetical source separate from the nine extant English versions.
notes: Smith finds no evidence that the English LFMB was composed much before the late-fourteenth-century B witness.
